entry.title: EWM Outb. delivery: Overdeliver on purpose without flag "unlimited overdelivery"
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are implementing a logic in our EWM that allows to either make an under- or overdeliver for an outbound delivery item on purpose. That means we want to use the precentage values "overdelivery" and "underdelivery" from the delivery item.

Right now it seems, that creating WTs that add up to more than the originally required quantity is not allowed if the flag "unlimited Overdelivery" (Field /SCDL/DL_DELTERM_STR-TOL_OVERUNLTD ) is NOT set in the delivery item, no matter if the percent values are populated. We do not want to use that flag in our deliveries but the percentage values instead, so we decide which quantity would be OK for the customer (e.g. -10%, + 10%). Does anybody have experience how to circumvent this flag (it is checked in include /SCWM/LTO_WHRF54, "IF ls_whr_item-delterm-tol_overunltd ... ) ? Is there a badi, user exit whatever? Thank you

entry.title: Re: Unable to Deselect "Goods Receipt" Checkbox in Config...
entry.content:
Hello @SaraG 

Thank you for your question regarding the Goods Receipt (GR) indicator for Account Assignment Category K (Cost Center) in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

It is a standard system behaviour.

  • The GR indicator in Configuration Activity 101815 is locked by design for Account Assignment K to ensure consistent procurement and reporting for Cost Centers.
  • Turning off "GR-Based Invoice Verification" in the Vendor Master affects invoice matching but does not disable the GR requirement itself.
  • Fully customising Account Assignment Categories or bypassing the GR requirement is not supported in S4 Public Cloud, only SAP-delivered options with limited flexibility can be used.
  • If you need to process purchases without a GR, you need to select an alternative Account Assignment Category, depending on your scope.

Reference: SAP Help – Account Assignment in Purchasing

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Why are certain Cost centers not getting replicated in Concur, however, they are available in SAP?
entry.content: We are using SAP ICS integration for Cost Center replication from SAP to Concur and noticed that all the cost centers available in SAP are not getting replicated to Concur. Possible Causes for missing Cost Center replication: Cost Centers which fall in any of the below categories will not be considered for export to Concur system: • Cost center is not valid at present (i.e. the start date is less than or equals today or the end date is greater than or equals today). • Cost centers are not part of the Phased Rollout selection criteria step of Cost Object export setup wizard. • Actual primary costs checkbox is checked in the SAP system, which means that the cost center is not relevant for financial posting. • Cost center name is not available in the Default language selected during the Specify Target System step of the System Setup wizard. Note: To customize the cost center export data, a BAdI “BADI_CTE_FIN_COBJ_DATA_CHANGE” can be implemented.

What Are Community Talks? 

Community Talks are short, 20-minute sessions where SAP customers, partners, and experts share practical experiences, technical solutions, and innovative ideas. entry.title: HCM Brasil: Crédito do Trabalhador – Portaria MTE 933/2025
entry.content:
Olá a todos, bem-vindos ao blog post com as atualizações sobre o Crédito do Trabalhador!

O governo publicou a Portaria MTE 933/2025 no dia 05/06/2025 permitindo que os trabalhadores possam contratar mais de um empréstimo do Crédito do Trabalhador vinculado ao mesmo empregador. Até essa publicação, era permitido apenas um contrato de empréstimo.

Essa portaria foi publicada com validade imediata impactando já a folha de pagamento de julho de 2025. Ou seja, no arquivo de empréstimos disponibilizado pelo governo no dia 21/06/2025, já podem constar trabalhadores com mais de um contrato de empréstimo.

 O que esperar da SAP?

A SAP já está atuando nas atualizações da solução para acomodar essa nova demanda, e a data de entrega está planejada para 18/07/2025. Vocês já podem acompanhar esse tópico no Regulatory Change Manager.

 O que já pode ser feito antes da entrega da SAP?

Para garantir que o empregado tenha recursos suficientes para o pagamento efetivo da parcela, o governo recomenda o provisionamento proporcional das parcelas nos adiantamentos de salário e férias. Como a solução já permite o cadastrado de mais de um contrato de empréstimo para o mesmo trabalhador, recomendamos que essa atividade seja realizada para que provisionamento dos valores seja realizado corretamente.

IMPORTANTE:

  • Caso haja mais de um contrato de empréstimo para o empregado no mesmo período, é necessário associar uma rubrica específica para cada contrato de empréstimo no infotipo Desconto de parcela em folha (3453). Recomendamos a cópia da rubrica modelo MTC0 - Parcela/créd. Trabalhador para cada empréstimo. 
  • O desconto efetivo das parcelas de empréstimo ocorre sempre nas folhas mensal e rescisão, e essas funcionalidades só estarão plenamente adaptadas a partir das entregas da SAP.

 Onde eu encontro mais informações sobre o Crédito do Trabalhador?

  • Portaria  MTE 933/2025 publicada no dia 05/06/2025.
  • Regulatory Change Manager.
  • Workzone de HCM Brasil.

 Esperamos que esse blog post tenha esclarecido as suas principais dúvidas sobre esse novo programa criado pelo governo.

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas.

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias.

Um abraço,        

Leandro D’Avila

entry.title: Is there a way to prevent a user from entering the same transaction more than twice?
entry.content:
Dear community

Is there a way to prevent a user from entering the same transaction more than twice?

Thank you for your help.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to prevent a user from entering the sa...
entry.content:
There is no general / generic feature that would prevent a user from entering the same transaction more than twice (and I believe that you mean in parallel).

Something that could be used to somehow achieve that is to limit the number of SAP GUI windows that a user can open with the profile parameter "rdisp/max_alt_modes".

Also keep in mind the possibility of parallel logons (parameter "login/disable_multi_gui_login"), as "rdisp/max_alt_modes" is applied per session (logon), not per user ID.

entry.title: LOG OF CHANGES TO USERS IN SAP S4HANA
entry.content:
I am encountering the following situation.
In SAP S4HANA I have not been able to identify in which tables the user change logs are stored in SU01.
For example, the last modification date of the user and which field was modified.

 

 

What I checked: the CDHDR and CDPOS tables do not store this type of modifications, for example: I modified a user's validation end field, and this is not stored in these tables.

Investigating I find the following: In S/4HANA:

Many changes are now handled via internal APIs, synchronization strategies and structures like SUID_ST_NODE_ADMINDATA.

Not all user changes are automatically reflected in CDHDR/CDPOS, even if they used to be reflected in ECC

SAP uses structures like SUID_ST_CHANGELOG, which is not a base table but is used in standard reports for traceability.

Anyone how can I access these change logs

Thanks.

entry.title: Recording Failure data for Proactive Maintenance work in the Perform Maintenance Jobs app
entry.content:
For the use case when a maintenance technician needs to record failure data during a proactive maintenance work and  there is no notification associated to the order, it can be enabled by maintaining configuration settings for Perform Maintenance Jobs app. This option automatically creates notification if a relevant notification is not been identified and technician would like to record failure. The feature was enabled in Cloud ERP 2408 release onwards. 

Following are the steps and details to enable it:

  • There is an additional setting in the ‘Define Control Parameters for Perform Maintenance Jobs app’ to enable the Create notification option for recording Malfunction” order type YA01 and YA02 . Notification type Y2 is defaulted for YA02 order type. The user need to provide Notification Priority.
  • This configuration is valid only when there is no notification could be identified and a new one needs to be created.

 

  • Once the configuration is maintained for the plant/order type, the ‘Malfunction Data’ tab will be available and maintenance technician will be able to maintain failure/as found data by clicking Edit button.
  • On save of malfunction data, system will create a new notification in the backend based on the priority defined in the configuration and will associate it appropriately.
  • The idea here is to record the failure data for the most relevant technical object for better analysis.

 

 

  • If the technical object is not maintained at job(operation/ sub-operation) level, but is available at the order header level and notification is associated at header level, failure data will be recorded in the header notification.
  • If there is no notification associated to the header level, system will create a new notification in the background and will record the failure data in the new notification.
  • If the technical object at job differs from the technical object at the order header level, it creates a new notification in the background when failure data is being recorded.
  • It will consider the header level notification only if the technical object at order header level and job level are same and no notification exists at job level.
  • Job level notification level takes precedence over the order header level notification.

entry.title: Explanation Document Splitting with Configuration Steps & Various Real Time Scenarios!
entry.content:
• Important questions arising while understanding document splitting

1. Why is document splitting used in enterprises and what benefits do companies get from document splitting?

Generally, the document is split as per the category assigned to general ledgers.

If the company wants a financial statement based on profit center, segment, or business area, then document splitting functionality is used to generate financial statements such as the balance sheet and profit & loss statement.

2. Key benefits a company gets from document splitting are:

Financial statements based on these three objects.

The company gets an overview/performance of profit centers, segments, or business areas if document splitting is active based on different objects.

It is helpful in important decision-making.

3. What is the main functionality of document splitting?

The main functionality of document splitting is to map general ledger as per different categories and split line items as per characteristics defined. There are three characteristics in general ledger accounting.

4. How many types of document splitting are there in SAP ERP? 

     There are three types of document splitting: 

  • Active Document Splitting: Active document splitting is done when we post vendor invoice/customer invoice, it splits document on basis of expense/income line items having profit center and payables/receivables line items are split according to the expense/income lines having profit center.
  • Passive Document Splitting: Passive document splitting is taking place when there are outgoing/incoming payment documents are posted; it splits document based on payables/receivables line items having profit center in vendor/customer invoice & bank line items splits on this basis. 
  • Zero-Balance Document Splitting: Zero balance document splitting is applicable to balance out debit or credit side of profit center and segment only. If there is an imbalance in profit center and segment, then zero-balance account is automatically generated setoff imbalance debit or credit side of line item. 

     

    5.  By default, document splitting will it get activated by SAP with configuring the              company code in the system or not? 

          Yes, it is true that document splitting is by default activated by SAP itself with configuring        the company code in the system and document splitting characteristics for general                  ledger are activated at client level. 

     

    Configurations Steps 

    Node- SPRO/SAP Reference IMG/Financial Accounting/General Ledger Accounting/Business Transaction/Document Splitting 

    1. Classify G/L Accounts for Document Splitting: General ledger account is classified as per different category. Here, general ledger accounts are derived from balance sheet or profit & loss statement can be known.  
    2. Classify Document Types for Document Splitting: Document types are classified for document splitting and here, business transaction and variant is assigned to each document type. If we created a new document type, then it is mandatory to classify that document type.  

  • (Note: There are many standard document types are already there in SAP & they are also classified)  

     

  • 3. Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting: Document splitting characteristics for general ledger accounting are defined if business wants financial statements based on objects. There are three objects available through which documents are splitting are done. 

  • Objects: 

  • Profit Center
  • Business Areas
  • Segments

    Here, in this image characteristic at profit center level is applied and another thing is Mandatory Field check box is marked due to this marking each line item has mandatory field for profit center. And Zero Balance check box gives remark that if it is marked then profit center must be balanced for both debit and credit side, so zero balance clearing account will automatically post if profit center is imbalanced.

  • 4. Define Zero-Balance Clearing Account: Zero balance clearing account is assigned in this transaction. 

  • Select standard for assignment  

     

    Assign account

     

     

    5. Activate Document Splitting: In this node we can activate or deactivate document splitting according to the requirement of the company.  

     

  • Note: In constant standard 0DFLT is selected 

    To deactivate this tab of deactivate 

     

    6. Edit Constant for Non assigned Processes:  
  •      Here, select default constants 

    And then click on assignment of values under dialog structure of independent constants, fill business area and segment as default. 

     

  • Now double click on Assignment of Profit Centers in Dialog Structure

  • Then assign profit center at controlling level set as default. If there is no assignment available in profit center field, then it will take from here. 

     

Real Time Scenarios of Types of Document Splitting 

Scenario-1 Active Document Splitting 

Entry View of Vendor Invoice with GST & TDS 

Explanation: In the above vendor invoice Expense 01 has profit center 1 & Expense 02 has profit center 2, here on basis of these two-line items another line items of GST, Trade payables and TDS also split in the ratio of 2:1 as per expense line items. In this manner each line item will get profit center automatically by the system & document splits when we click on general ledger view. 

In this way active document splitting is applicable to vendor invoices and same as in customer invoices. 

General Ledger View of Vendor Invoice

Above simulation of general ledger view of vendor invoice after active document splitting is applicable. 

 

Scenario-2 Passive Document Splitting 

Entry View of Outgoing Payment Document

Explanation: At the time of outgoing payment & incoming payment of vendor invoices and customer invoices only passive document is applicable and here, document line items of outgoing bank, cash discount received & trade payables are split as per trade payables line items ratio of vendor invoice, another point is no profit center is entered it will be capture automatically by the system from vendor invoice trade payables line items ratio in 2:1. 

 

General Ledger View of Outgoing Payment Document 

Simulation of outgoing payment passive document splitting. 

(Note: In passive splitting cash discount line items splits in entry view and get two different line-item numbers & it will get item number as per the number of profit centers in vendor invoice.) 

 

Scenario-3 Zero-Balance Document Splitting 

Entry View of General Ledger Document 

In the general ledger document, there are three-line items with three different profit centers and Expense 01 debited has profit center 1, Expense 02 credited has profit center 2 & Expense 03 debited has profit center 3. 

In this document profit centers are imbalanced. 

Line ItemsProfit CenterProfit Center DebitProfit Center Credit
Expense-01 Debited11N/A
Expense-02 Credited2N/A2
Expense-03 Debited33N/A

The above table shows missing profit centers either in debit balance or credit balance. 

To correct this imbalance in the profit center zero-balance clearing line items automatically generated by the system and fulfill missing profit center balance and its amount also filled as per line item of expense. 

This is how zero-balancing document splitting works. 

 

General Ledger View of GL Document 

Simulation of general ledger document in general ledger view zero balance clearing line items fulfills missing debit or credit side of profit center and this same scenario is applicable when defined characteristics is segment or business area. 

 

 

 

Thank you for reading & hope it will make ease in understanding.

With Best Regards

entry.title: Understanding Automatic Payment Program Configuration.
entry.content:
> What is the Automatic Payment Program (APP) in SAP?

The Automatic Payment Program (APP) is one of the most powerful and widely used tools in SAP Financial Accounting (FI). It's used to automate the process of paying open items (like vendor invoices, customer refunds, or employee reimbursements) using pre-defined rules and parameters.

Instead of manually processing each payment, APP streamlines this by selecting due items and even creating bank files (for RTGS, NEFT, etc.) ready to be sent to the bank.

> Why is APP used in real businesses?

In large organizations, it’s common to have hundreds or thousands of transactions daily. Doing all this manually can lead to:

  • Human errors

  • Missed due dates

  • Loss of cash discounts

So, because of all these points, the APP is used in Companies.

> Basic Configuration Of Automatic Payment Program (APP)

        Open T code FTZP 

           1) Select 1st Option ( All Company Codes)

Special Gl :-

A = Downpayment 

F = Downpayment Request

P = Payment Request 

2) Paying Company Codes

For Forms field :

Press F4 then select Payment Medium > International > Payment Medium Workbench > Payment Advice Note (International)

3) Payment Method In C/R

 

Create 3 basic Payment methods for your country:-

C = Cheque (Check)

E = Cash Payment

T = Bank Transfer

Under each Payment Method, you can choose a payment method either for Outgoing Payment or Incoming Payment, or both. 

Tick on Payment Method Classification according to your Payment Method.

After creating and saving your Payment Method, select your payment method and click on currencies allowed ( on the left side, 2nd option), make New entries  > and mention your country's currency.

4) Payment Methods in Company Code

You have created all types of payment methods in step 3 for a specific country. Now we have to maintain that in our company code. 

 

This configuration tells SAP:

  • What type of payments are allowed in that company code

  • What payment method applies to vendors/customers

  • What accounts, forms, and processes should be used when making a payment

Put the Maximum and Minimum amount limit on your payment method.

 

5) Bank Determination

(Before going to this option, 1st create a House Bank using Fiori (/N/UI2/FLP). Use the FIORI app "Manage Banks" to configure the House bank.

After selecting your paying company code,

  • Select Ranking Order:-

  •  

Put your ranking order of your bank according to the payment method, so the system will understand which bank to use for payment, and the payment method. If the funds are not sufficient in the 1st bank according to your ranking, the system will check for 2nd bank if any ..

 

  • Bank Accounts

Here you have to maintain your GL accounts of your bank ( usually we create 3 GL for one House bank, which are Main Bank Account, Incoming Bank Account, and Outgoing Bank Account.)

Put your House Bank, Payment Method, and select the GL ( Most of the time it is Outgoing Bank Account)

 

  • Bank Accounts (Enhanced)

Same as above, but more details are required to fill.

 

  • Available Amounts

The Available Amount setting is crucial. It defines the maximum amount that can be paid from a specific bank account per day during automatic payments. ( In short, it is a balance of the bank account that the end user needs to update daily )

 

Here we finish the basics of  APP configuration.

entry.title: Building a Seamless Onboarding Experience for Global Assignees
entry.content:
A global assignment refers to the process of sending an employee from one country to work in another as part of an organization’s global workforce strategy. It is a key component of global mobility programs used by multinational companies.  A Global Assignment can be Short-term (a few months to a year) or Long-term (1–2 years).

With the latest 2H 2025 release, SuccessFactors Onboarding supports the Global Assignment process, along with support for country/region specific forms in the host country. 

With the Global Assignment, you can send an employee on a global assignment. The administrator or manager can add a global assignment for an employee. Once the global assignment is active, there will one home and one host employment. The employee will be onboarded on new employment (host employment).

Process flow: Add Global Assignment:

Onboarding currently supports initiating the Global Assignment from Employee Central only. Once onboarding is initiated, the regular onboarding process is followed:

1. Review New Hire Data: Employment-related data added during the creation of the global assignment needs to be reviewed by the employer.
2. New Hire Tasks:  Welcome mail, assign buddy, schedule meetings, goals, equipment, recommend links and people, etc.
3. Personal Data Collection:  Update personal data when moved to new employment. For e.g. Contact, National ID
4. Additional Data Collection: Employees can additional data required for host country, based on company policies.
5. Compliance forms: Complete compliance forms and signatures based on host country requirements.

The Host country/region compliance forms assignment is based on the hostcountry and hoststate provided during personal data collection for the Address portlet. This is the default compliance-filtering rule for assigning the forms in case of global assignment.

It is possible to override the default configuration with the help of FieldMetaOverrideConfiguration object configuration. 

Since process in initiated from Employee Central, any changes to Start Date or Hiring manager will be reflected in the onboarding process. Similarly, ending of global assignment, cancels the onboarding process. 

Employers can reduce paperwork, offer personalized onboarding experience for employees in the host country and adhere to local regulations.

entry.title: Streamline collaboration in Engineer-to-Order Projects with SAP Project and Resource Management
entry.content:
Challenges in Managing Customer ETO Projects

Manufacturing companies encounter several distinct challenges when managing engineer-to-order (ETO) projects. Firstly, complex coordination is required as multiple internal and external partners must collaborate effectively and synchronize their efforts. Additionally, dynamic timelines pose a significant challenge; any changes in project schedules can have widespread repercussions throughout the entire supply chain. Communication gaps are also problematic, as ensuring that all stakeholders remain aligned and well-informed can be a cumbersome task. Lastly, resource allocation is a critical issue, as resources must be managed efficiently across various projects to maintain productivity and project completion timelines

The Need for Efficient Collaboration

To overcome these challenges, manufacturing companies need a robust platform that facilitates efficient collaboration. SAP Project and Resource Management, a cloud solution built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), is designed to provide project managers with the capabilities to plan, monitor, and communicate effectively with both internal and external partners.

Image: SAP Project and Resource Management extending SAP Cloud ERP with Project Management Capabilities 

Integrated with SAP Cloud ERP it allows you to easily extract project information from your ERP system, share it with all project parties, trigger project changes and approvals and plan risk mitigation activities.  Let's illustrate the usage of SAP Project and Resource Management with a real-life example.

Meet Oscar - The Project Manager

Imagine Oscar, a dedicated project manager responsible for delivering a new processing line for a valued customer. In a meeting with the customer Oscar was informed about the need to shift the project timeline by 4 weeks as the plant is blocked by other work. He needs to inform the contractor responsible for installing essential equipment about the new timelines.

In the past, this task could have been daunting. But with SAP Project and Resource Management , Oscar has everything he needs at his fingertips. With a few clicks he can import the current project plan from an ERP system such as SAP Cloud ERP into SAP Project and Resource Management . In the GANTT chart he adjusts the project timeline with the mouse pointer. He uses then the built-in scheduling capabilities to re-schedules the entire project according to task dependencies and date constraints.

Image: Schedule project uploaded from SAP Cloud ERP 

Meet Stacy - The Project Contractor 

As a next steps Oscar invites Stacy, the contractor, to the project collaboration space and assigns her the task of reviewing the updated schedule. SAP Project and Resource Management provides an embedded invitation process, which makes it easy for Oscar to onboard new project partners and team members.  After final registration stacey is ready to access shared project information. 

Image: Invite partner Stacy to collaboration project

Stacy carefully examines the new timeline and notices a potential delay in equipment delivery. Using SAP Project and Resource Management  Stacy documents this discrepancy as a project issue including information about possible alternative components and timelines. Once Stacy completed the creation of the project issue,  the software automatically notifies Oscar via email.

Image: Stacy creates project issue to inform Oscar about a delay 

Integrated 3D Visualization for Enhanced Efficiency

One of the standout features of SAP PRM is its integrated 3D visualization. With the help of digital twin data, Oscar can quickly identify the project components and tasks affected by the delay. Using the 3D model of the plant he can check if other nearby components are affected by the change, access additional engineering information and plan mitigation activities directly from the the visual representation.

Image: Use of  3D visualization to investigate project issues and mitigation activities 

With just a few clicks, he is able to approve the necessary changes to resolve the issue, streamlining the project and enhancing cost efficiency. Approvals are documented in the project collaboration platform and all project partners are notified about the new changes.

Image: Approve project changes 

Benefits of Using SAP Project and Resource Management

SAP Project and Resource Management offers a multitude of benefits that can significantly enhance project management efficiency:

  1. Seamless Collaboration: Facilitates effective communication and coordination among all project stakeholders.
  2. Real-Time Insights: Provides real-time visibility into project status and issues, ensuring timely decision-making.
  3. Cost Efficiency: Enhances resource allocation and cost management, minimizing delays and overruns.
  4. Improved Project Outcomes: Ensures projects are delivered within scope, on time, and within budget.

Empower Your Project Management with SAP PRM

SAP Project and Resource Management is a game-changer for effective project management. By leveraging digital twin information with active project and task management, this software elevates project efficiency to a whole new level. It empowers project managers like Oscar to address project issues proactively, minimize delays, and ensure successful project delivery.

Discover how SAP Project and Resource Management  can transform the way you collaborate with project partners. Visit www.sap.com/prm for more information and take the first step towards streamlined, efficient, and collaborative project management.

Additional Resources: 

- SAP Community Page for SAP Project and Resource Management 

- Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502

- SAP Help Portal - SAP Project and Resource Management

entry.title: Automatic Assignment of Resource and Carrier in the Freight Order for Short Cut Planning
entry.content:
Shortcut Planning - In high-volume logistics environments, efficiency hinges on minimizing manual intervention. One such opportunity lies in automating the assignment of resources and carriers during Freight Order (FO) creation, especially in shortcut planning scenarios within SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management (TM). Shortcut planning in S/4HANA TM refers to a simplified planning approach where Freight Orders are created directly.

Limitation of Shortcut planning - When the Freight Order is created using shortcut planning, the resource is not assigned to the Freight Order, which can further be used in the end-to-end processing of integration scenarios with other modules, e.g., Last Mile Distribution. In such a situation, the vehicle needs to be assigned manually in the Freight Order.

In this blog, I am covering the configurations for the automatic assignment of the vehicle resource along with the carrier for the Freight Order created from the enhanced shortcut planning. While there are alternative methods for carrier assignment, this blog focuses on using the standard strategy. There is another way for carrier assignment for Freight Order, but in this blog using the standard strategy. From S/4HANA 2023 (Service Pack SAPK-90203INUIAPFI70), there is another setting Carrier Determination Profile which can be assigned to Freight Unit and Freight Order type for the assignment of carriers based on source location, source zone, destination location, or destination zone combination.

Configurations for Vehicle Resource and Carrier Assignment for Shortcut Planning Scenario

1) Define Condition Types : 

Defining a condition type to be used for the determination of the resource under SPRO → Transportation Management → Basic Functions → Condition → Define Condition Types

Condition Type

Condition Type Description

Name of the Structure

Business Object

BO Node Name

Condition Result from BRFplus Is Struct.

One Condition for this Cond. Type

Z_TO_RES_ASGN

Determination for Resource Name

/SCMTMS/RES_NAME

/SCMTMS/TOR

ROOT

  

2) Creating a Condition using the condition types defined in the previous step : 

Business Document Ca

Business Document Ty

Resource

=TO

=ZF01

<<Resource Name>>

3) Define Service

Path : SPRO → Transportation Management → Basic Functions → Process Controller → Define Strategy

Standard Service used → TOR_CREATE (TM Change Controller for Creation (Asynchronous)

4) Define Strategy

Defined  the strategy for the resource and carrier assignment

Path : SPRO → Transportation Management → Basic Functions → Process Controller → Define Strategy

StrategyServiceDescription
ZTM_CREATETOR_CREATEStrategy for Resource & Carrier Assignment 
 

5) Define Methods

Using the standard methods for the resource and carrier assignment

Path : SPRO → Transportation Management → Basic Functions → Process Controller → Define Method

 
MethodServiceDescriptionMethod TypeInterface ComponentClass/Interface
DET_VEHTOR_CREATEResource DeterminationBDET_VEHICLE_RES/SCMTMS/CL_CREATE_SAVE_METHODS
CARR_SELTOR_CREATECarrier SelectionBCARRIER_SELECTION/SCMTMS/CL_CREATE_SAVE_METHODS

 

6) Assign Methods to a Strategy

Assign the methods that have been created in the previous steps to the strategy

Path : SPRO → Transportation Management → Basic Functions → Process Controller → Assign Methods to a Strategy

StrategyMethodSequenceDescriptionDescription
ZTM_CREATEDET_VEH10Strategy for Resource & Carrier AssignmentVehicle Resource Determination
ZTM_CREATECARR_SEL20Strategy for Resource & Carrier AssignmentCarrier Assignment

7) Define Parameters

Path : SPRO → Transportation Management → Basic Functions → Process Controller → Define Parameters

Parameter NameServiceParam. TypeData Element
COND_IDTOR_CREATES-Character Sting/SCMTMS/CONDITION_ID

8 Assign Parameters to a Method

Assigning the parameters to the methods that have been created in the previous steps to the strategy

Path : SPRO → Transportation Management → Basic Functions → Process Controller → Assign Parameters to a Method

MethodParameter Name
COND_IDTOR_CREATE

9) Assign Method Parameters to a Strategy

Assigning the method parameters to the strategy that have been created in the previous steps to the strategy under SPRO → Transportation Management → Basic Functions → Process Controller → Assign Method Parameters to a Strategy

StrategyMethodParameter NameDescriptionDescriptionParam. TypeINT4 Text Field
ZTM_CREATEDET_VEHCOND_IDVehicle Resource DeterminationStrategy for Resource & Carrier Assignment S00Z_TOR_RESOURCE_ASSIGNMENT

10) Creation of Carrier Selection Setting

A carrier selection setting has been created for assigning in the Freight Order type: In the carrier selection setting, a strategy to consider the lane master has been specified. For this testing, one carrier has been assigned in the lane master.

11) Assignment of Create Strategy and Carrier Selection Setting in the Freight Order Type

 

FO TypeDescription of Document TypeStrategy for CreationDefault Carrier Selection Settings
ZF01Default Freight OrderZTM_CREATEZCAR_SELECTION_SETTING

Testing of the scenario

Delivery has been created and automatically Freight Unit and Freight Order got created. Vehicle resource and carrier got assigned in the Freight Order.

entry.title: Cloning a Corporate GitHub Repository (Python) to VS Code on MAC
entry.content:
Working in a corporate environment often means using private repositories hosted on GitHub Enterprise. If you're on a Mac and prefer the Terminal, here’s a simple step-by-step guide to clone the repo and get started with VS Code.

Prerequisites

  • Homebrew installed
  • Git installed - or run the following command from terminal
brew install git​
  • Visual Studio Code installed
  • Access to your company’s GitHub (e.g., GitHub Enterprise or another corporate Git)
  • Make sure you have a personal access token; if not, generate a new one

 

  • UV installed: UV is a Python package/dependency manager; install it using
brew install uv​

Steps

1. Verify Git is installed

which git
git --version​

2. Install GitHub Repositories Extension in VS Code

3. Clone the Repository via Terminal

git clone https://github.company.com/your-team/your-repo.git​


If prompted, enter your GitHub username and your Personal Access Token instead of a password.

4. Change into the Cloned Directory

cd your-repo​

5. Open the Folder in VS Code

code .


If code is not recognized, open VS Code → Cmd + Shift + P → “Shell Command: Install 'code' command in PATH”.

6. Install Python Extensions in VS Code

If you're working on a Python project, consider installing the following VS Code extensions:

  • Python
  • Jupyter
  • Black Formatter

7. Install Python Project Dependencies with uv


In the VS Code terminal:

uv sync

entry.title: How to Enable S/4 HANA Outbound Service for Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous)
entry.content:
This blog post provides a comprehensive guide on enabling outbound SOAP service for asynchronous journal entry posting, specifically utilizing the CO_FINS_JOURNAL_ENTRY_BULK_CRE service.

Essential Notes for Upgrading to S/4HANA 2023

For users who upgrade to S/4HANA 2023, it is essential to implement the following notes that enhance the restart process and manage outbound messages. Additionally, maintaining the Application Interface Framework (AIF) configuration is a crucial manual step that must be completed for receiving the outbound messages.

  • 3325557(Required) : /AIF/ERR: 'Restart' button behavior not correct for interface /FINAC/JRNLENTRIN&JELDGR_IN
  • 3438627(Required) : AIF Content for SOAP Journal Entry API (Scenario ID: SAP_COM_0002)
  • 3477010(Required) : API of Journal Entry – Post (Asynchronous): No Journal Entry Confirmation and Message (Scenario ID: SAP_COM_0002)
  • 3527800 (Optional) : Journal Entry Post API: Improve performance for outbound messages
  • 3568403(Optional): Journal Entry Post by Ledger: Improve performance for outbound messages

Service Setup Process

This diagram shows that a payload is sent from system A to a S/4HANA system B. Journal entries are created in system B. After that, the journal entry numbers are sent back to system B for further process. If you have already prepared the complete URL of the service in system B, proceed with the Web Service Configuration Steps below. If not, you can follow the Create Your Own Service on S/4 HANA first.

Create Your Own Service on S/4 HANA

1. Download WSDL file of outbound service JournalEntryBulkCreationRequest_Out

  1.  

  2. Create a customized inbound service based on the WSDL file

       2.1 Tcode: SPROXY

            

      2.2 Click Continue and select ‘External WSDL/Schema’, then WSDL file from your local file, and give an appropriate name of the service.

         

   2.3 Your customized service is created. Add your logic in the implementing class.

                 

3. Run tcode SOAMANAGER, and click Web Service Configuration,  find your customized service definition and create a new service binding.

Note down the completed URL, which combines Host and calculated access URL

Web Service Configuration Steps

1. Run tcode: SOAMANAGER,  click Web Service Configuration, and find out the standard consumer proxy CO_FINS_JOURNAL_ENTRY_BULK_CRE. Create a new logic port under it.

Note:  The URL is generated from the first. If you have created your own service, you can also link it here.

 

2. Map logic port to system id in maintenance view FINSV_JE_MAPOUT

Test steps:

You can test the Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous) via Postman. Don’t forget to add SenderBusinessSystemID. Otherwise the outbound service is not triggered.

entry.title: Extract Workday® HCM data into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Once again, we are expanding our offering to analyze processes that touch non-SAP system – this time for Human Resources. We are happy to announce the release of our new integration package content for Workday® Human Capital Management which allows you to extract data from Workday® HCM into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

Improve Your Human Resource Process Analysis

Improved and streamlined HR processes have a positive lasting impact on your business efficiency and benefits your workforce. The new integration content for Workday® HCM and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence is an essential starting point and enables you to visualize, analyze, and get fast insights into your Attract to Acquire process.

Access the Integration Content on Business Accelerator Hub

Let’s dive into the topic and start with where you can find the integration content.

Logon to SAP Business Accelerator Hub and under "Explore" search for "SAP Signavio": 

 

Scroll down until you see the “Discover Integrations” area and search for Workday®.

 

Click on the Workday® tile. There you can find the available Workday® integration package for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. 

Click on the integration package.

 

You see a list of integration flows highlighting which business objects and events are being extracted. This includes the business objects "Job Application", "Employee Contract", "Candidate", and many more.

For further details on the underlying data extraction logic from Workday HCM, how it is transformed and pushed into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – you can open the respective integration flows.

 

Also check out the configuration guide attached to the integration package.

The integration package includes dedicated integration flows that outline the relevant business objects and events extracted by default. This setup enables a quick start with the flexibility to adjust and extend the content to meet your specific business needs.

 

Download content into SAP Integration Suite

To use the content, log on to SAP Integration Suite and go to “Discover” and click on the "Integrations" section. 

 

Use the search bar and search for "Workday", you will find all available content relating to Workday integration, including our new package for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence:

 

If you check out the package "Workday Integration with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence", you can find details on all available artifacts on the integration scenarios. To use the package, simply click on "copy" on the top right corner. This will automatically copy this integration package into your design area for further configuration and deployment.

 

Key Benefits of the Integration

  • Comprehensive Process Visibility: Achieve end-to-end transparency across your hiring operations, enabling the identification of process inefficiencies and areas for improvement.
  • Customizable Data Extraction: The predefined business objects and events can be tailored to your organization’s business requirements, offering adaptability for your specific use cases.
  • Enhanced Decision-Making: Seamlessly integrate accurate process execution data from Workday HCM into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to support evidence-based decision-making.

 

Share Your Feedback

We are looking forward to hear your thoughts and feedback on the new integration package. Share your experiences and suggestions in the comments section below.

In case you missed it – check out the available integration content for Salesforce (Blog post / Integration content), Microsoft Dynamics CRM (Blog post / Integration content) and Coupa (Blog post / Integration content).

entry.title: Help influence the next AI Features for My Home on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
My Home is the starting point for SAP S/4HANA. It contains all your to-do items, news feeds, apps, and insights tiles and cards. Artificial intelligence is available throughout the page, including alerts to your to-dos, recommended apps, based on your role, and the ability to create new cards using natural language. Of course, Joule is also available to help you find the information you need to run your business.

We are continuing to enhance the capabilities in My Home and would love to hear your feedback on two powerful enhancements designed to improve usability and streamline everyday tasks in SAP applications. To collect your ideas, we are running two new customer engagement initiatives. These are open to customers and partners running SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

This initiative focuses on:

  • AI-assisted Smart Visualization in My Home – Situations, which provides resolution suggestions directly on the homepage for faster task completion (https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3931)
  • AI-powered error resolution, including natural language input and intelligent suggestions to help users fix system errors quickly and intuitively (https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3929)

These innovations aim to reduce training time, boost productivity, and make the SAP experience more seamless and intelligent.

SAP Products in Focus

  • My Home page
  • SAP Fiori apps using the SAP Fiori elements framework

Why Join?

  • Get early access to upcoming innovations
  • Help shape tools that better fit your day-to-day needs
  • Collaborate with SAP designers, developers, and product experts
  • Influence real product enhancements with your feedback

 Your input now will help improving the user experience of SAP Cloud ERP, helping other customers save time and simplify business processes.

entry.title: How to Collect a HTTP Archive (HAR file) from the SAC Mobile App: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Ensuring the smooth performance of the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) mobile app is essential for maintaining user satisfaction and productivity. While you might be familiar with troubleshooting network performance issues in desktop environments, the mobile landscape presents its own unique challenges. One effective way to identify and address performance bottlenecks in the SAC mobile app is by collecting and analyzing a HTTP Archive (HAR file).

With this blog, I am expanding upon and further elaborating on some of the points mentioned in my previous blog - Strategy for troubleshooting network performance issues in SAP Analytics Cloud. 

Why Collect a HAR File? 

A HAR file contains detailed information about network requests and responses, including timings, headers, and content. This data is invaluable for diagnosing performance issues and understanding how the app interacts with backend services.

Prerequisites

This guide uses Fiddler Classic for Windows as an example to capture traffic from iOS Device, but another a web debugging proxy tool with similar functionality can also be used to collect the HAR file.  If you're looking for Fiddler for macOS, use Fiddler Everywhere or use virtualization products like VMWare Fusion or Parallels Desktop to run Fiddler Classic in a virtual machine on your Mac.  

Step-by-Step Guide

1. Install and Configure Fiddler.
1.1 Download and Install Fiddler:
  • Fiddler Classic: Download and install Fiddler Classic from the official website Telerik.
  • Fiddler Everywhere: Download and install Fiddler Everywhere from the official website Telerik
1.2. Configure Fiddler to Capture Traffic:
  • Fiddler Classic :
    • Follow steps in Capture Traffic from iOS Device.
    • Follow steps in Configure Fiddler Classic for Android Device.
  • Fiddler Everywhere: 
    • Follow steps in Capturing iOS Traffic - Fiddler Everywhere.
    • Follow steps in Capturing Android Traffic - Fiddler Everywhere.
2. Setting Up Your Mobile Device

2.1 Connect Your Mobile Device and Computer to the Same Network:

  • Ensure that both your mobile device and the computer running Fiddler are connected to the same Wi-Fi network.

2.2 Configure Proxy Settings and Trust Fiddler's Root Certificate on Your Mobile Device:

  • Fiddler Classic:
    • Follow steps in Set the iOS Device Proxy on iOS Device.
    • Follow steps in Configure Android Device on Android Device.
  • Fiddler Everywhere:
    • Follow steps in Configuring the Fiddler proxy on iOS Device.
    • Follow steps in Configuring the Fiddler proxy on Android Device.
3. Capturing and Analyzing Network Traffic (Fiddler Classic)

3.1. Start Capturing Traffic:

  • In Fiddler Classic, go to Fiddler's "File" menu, click the "Capture Traffic" button to start capturing network traffic:

  • Open SAC mobile app and SAC Story:

  • All HTTP/HTTPS traffic should now be captured by Fiddler Classic: 

3.2. Analyze the Captured Traffic:
  • Click on a request to see detailed information, including headers, request and response bodies, and timing information: 

  • Once the required traffic is captured it can ben be exported in various formats including HAR File. To do this  go to Fiddler's "File" menu, select "Export Sessions" > "All Sessions", and save the output as a "HTTPArchive v1.2":

Conclusion

By following these steps, you can collect a HAR file from the SAC mobile app, gaining detailed insights into network performance. This information can be used to identify and troubleshoot performance issues, ultimately helping you optimize the app's performance for mobile users.

Call to Action

If you encounter persistent issues or need additional assistance, don't hesitate to reach out to your Technical Quality Manager (TQM), your SAP Account, your Customer Success Partner (CSP), or our central entry channel, the Virtual Project Room (VPR). They can help you leverage support from SAP's Center of Expertise (COE) for Analytics, Planning, and Consolidation.

entry.title: Inverted IDs
entry.content:
Hi community,

We are currently implementing the Employee Central module in a system that already has Employee Profile configured, since it uses other Talent modules and the information comes from SAP HCM.


We noticed that the data currently in SuccessFactors is swapped: the employee’s Person ID (PERSONID_EXT in SAP) is being sent to the User ID field in SuccessFactors, and the User ID (PERNR in SAP) is being sent to the EmpID field.


Has this ever happened to you?


Could you help us out?

Thank you very much,

entry.title: Product Inspiration Series - Experience a new Era of SAP CX (wave #3)

16:59 - Demo: Create a Multi-Day Visit Plan
17:35 - Demo: Inspect Visit Lists and optimized Routes
21:17 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

2. OneView Commerce Store Fulfillment for SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP OMS in Action (coming soon)

Nicholas Flowers, Chief Product Officer at OneView Commerce, provides an overview on OneView Commerce Store Fulfillment capabilities for SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP Order Management Services. Watch this product inspiration video to learn how OneView Commerce integrates seamlessly with SAP CX to enhance store fulfillment and clienteling experiences. Nicholas discusses the strategic vision behind the partnership, highlighting the need to modernize in-store POS systems and leverage AI for personalized customer journeys. He shares real-world examples of how OneView Commerce and SAP are transforming retail, from increasing in-store basket sizes and repeat visits to optimizing inventory utilization. Additionally, Nicholas offers insights into the future of AI in customer experience, emphasizing the importance of human interaction and real-time data pipelines. Don't miss the demo showcasing the unified capabilities of OneView Commerce and SAP CX, from order details and picking & packing to clienteling features like product search, customer profiles, and shopping cart management.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:26 - Why OneView Commerce and SAP?
04:57 - Which specific advantages do SAP customers get from OneView Commerce?
08:09 - Which role does AI play for OneView Commerce?
12:26 - OneView Commerce and SAP CX unify customer journeys across digital and physical channels
14:27 - Start of the demo
15:15 - Demo: Store Fulfillment - Instore Associate sees all Order Details
15:36 - Demo: Store Fulfillment - Instore Associate starts Picking & Packing
16:12 - Demo: Store Fulfillment - Instore Associate prints Label for Customer Order
17:36 - Demo: Clientelling - Product Search
18:43 - Demo: Clientelling - Customer Profile with Rewards, Purchase History and Recommended Products
19:21 - Demo: Clientelling - Shopping Cart
20:54 - Demo: Clientelling - Alert Center
23:45 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

3. Anaphora Content Studio for SAP Emarsys in Action (coming soon)

Andreas Svensson, Co-Founder and CTO of Anaphora, and Hanna Gustafsson, UX Lead Product, provide an overview on Anaphora Content Studio and its integration with SAP Emarsys. Watch this product inspiration video to learn how Anaphora Content Studio enhances customer experiences and multichannel engagement through seamless SAP CX integration. Discover the advantages, AI vision, and real-world customer stories, such as Porsche's successful brand refresh and new car launches. See firsthand how the platform supports multiple communication channels, ensures brand consistency at scale, and leverages AI for intelligently governed content creation. This demo showcases the innovative capabilities that empower businesses to elevate their customer experiences and achieve tangible results.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:26 - Why Anaphora and SAP CX?
02:20 - Can you share a customer story showcasing Anaphora use cases with SAP CX?
04:17 - What specific advantages do customers get from Anaphora Content Studio?
05:30 - What is your vision for AI in Anaphora Content Studio?
07:13 - Start of the demo
08:14 - Demo: Supporting multiple communication channels and multiple accounts
09:24 - Demo: Create email templates
09:59 - Demo: Design with the content editor
12:34 - Demo: Transfer and distribute templates
13:50 - Demo: Brand consistency at scale
14:22 - Demo: Posts in the My Porsche app
14:44 - Recap: Anaphora and SAP Emarsys
15:31 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

Enjoy and please reach out if you have any questions.

 

Back to the Product Inspiration Series 2025  |  Wave I   |  Wave II  |  Wave III

However, as of now it supports only MS Sharepoint, AWS S3 and SFTP; this post intends to highlight one of the use cases where the sensitive legal documents might be stored on other repos such as Google Cloud Storage, Google Drive for Business, MS OneDrive for Business, Dropbox Business, Nextcloud, ownCloud, NAS, Azure Blob etc. I will update the post accordingly.

entry.title: Modify statement in a determination giving short dump, going in infinite loop.
entry.content:
I have a single determination in my behavior definition as follows,

determination setCountryCode on save { field Country; create; update; }

following is the implementation in behavior pool,

CLASS lhc_ZI_INVESTOR DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS get_global_authorizations FOR GLOBAL AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR zi_investor RESULT result.

    METHODS setcountrycode FOR DETERMINE ON save
      IMPORTING keys FOR zi_investor~setcountrycode.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZI_INVESTOR IMPLEMENTATION.

METHOD setCountryCode.


    READ ENTITIES OF zi_investor IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY zi_investor
        FIELDS ( Country Mobile )
        WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_mobile).

    IF lt_mobile IS NOT INITIAL.

      LOOP AT lt_mobile ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_mobile>).

        IF <fs_mobile>-Country = 'India'.
          <fs_mobile>-Mobile = |+91 | & |{ <fs_mobile>-Mobile }|.
        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.

    ENDIF.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_investor IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY zi_investor
        UPDATE FIELDS ( Mobile )
        WITH VALUE #( FOR item IN lt_mobile ( %tky = item-%tky Mobile = item-Mobile ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

This modify statement at the end is somehow going in an infinte loop, how can I fix this, I saw one post but there, two determinations were calling each other, but here I only have a single determination, I also have a validation but that should not be relevant, please help, thanks.
