entry.title: Re: Program for KSB1 to run multiple variants together
entry.content:
Thanks Raymond/Deepak.

Actually we want to run this program same as KSB1 with only difference of running multiple variants & able to download in xlsx & pdf both.For many of our variants layouts are different (different fields)? Can we pull those fields only which are mentioned in variant (layout) or do we need to fix the fields? We were able to achieve output by fixing fields (meaning all variants will use same fields irrespective of layout). Now the problem here is we want to ‘Bold’ subtotals & totals line. I saw this is possible with ABAP2XLSX (from GitHub) however we don’t want to install any open source in our system. The other option I saw is OLE however I think it only works with GUI but not Fiori & we want it to work in GUI as well as Fiori. Any other option we have? I have included the code if you can help.

REPORT z_Test.

TABLES: sscrfields, cobk, kaep_coac.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_final,
         bukrs    TYPE bukrs,
         refbn    TYPE co_refbn,
         kstar    TYPE kstar,
         kostl    TYPE kostl,
         cel_ktxt TYPE kaep_coac-cel_ktxt,
         bltxt    TYPE co_bltxt,
         mat_txt  type maktx,            "mat description
         sgtxt    TYPE co_sgtxt,
         ebeln    type ebeln,
         budat    TYPE budat,
         WOVBTR   type string,            "kaep_coac-wovbtr
         "perio    TYPE co_perio,
         "wkgbtr   TYPE string,         "kaep_coac-wkgbtr,
         "cpudt    TYPE cpudt,
         "kwaer    TYPE kwaer,
       END OF ty_final.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_final_pdf,                        " for pdf structure
         bukrs    TYPE bukrs,
         refbn    TYPE co_refbn,
         kstar    TYPE kstar,
         kostl    TYPE kostl,
         cel_ktxt TYPE kaep_coac-cel_ktxt,
         bltxt    TYPE co_bltxt,
         mat_txt  type maktx,
         sgtxt    TYPE co_sgtxt,
         ebeln    type ebeln,
         budat    TYPE budat,
         wovbtr   type kaep_coac-wovbtr,
*         perio    TYPE co_perio,
*         wkgbtr   TYPE kaep_coac-wkgbtr,          "changed here
*         cpudt    TYPE cpudt,
*         kwaer    TYPE kwaer,
       END OF ty_final_pdf.


DATA: lt_final     TYPE TABLE OF ty_final,
      wa_final     TYPE ty_final,
      lt_final_pdf TYPE TABLE OF ty_final_pdf,
      wa_final_pdf TYPE ty_final_pdf.

DATA:
  lv_path_xlsx TYPE string,
  lv_path_pdf  TYPE string,                     " Local file for upload/download
  lv_variant   TYPE rsvar-variant,
  lv_var       TYPE rsvar-variant.                 "used in selection screen

DATA: lt_output TYPE REF TO data.
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lt_data> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
               <ls_row>  TYPE any.

DATA: lv_amount_str TYPE string,
      lv_amount     TYPE string. "kaep_coac-wkgbtr..

CONSTANTS : c_x  TYPE c VALUE 'X'.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
  SELECT-OPTIONS: s_var FOR lv_var,
                  s_budat FOR cobk-budat.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b2 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-015.
  PARAMETERS: r_fg RADIOBUTTON GROUP r2 DEFAULT 'X' USER-COMMAND u1 MODIF ID dd5,
              r_bg RADIOBUTTON GROUP r2 MODIF ID dd5.
  PARAMETERS: p_path  TYPE localfile LOWER CASE MODIF ID id2.
  PARAMETERS: p_email TYPE ad_smtpadr MODIF ID id1.
  SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b3 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-002.
    PARAMETERS: p_xlsx AS CHECKBOX DEFAULT 'X',
                p_pdf  AS CHECKBOX.
  SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b3.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b2.


AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_path.
  PERFORM get_file_path.


AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    CASE screen-group1.
      WHEN 'ID1'.
        screen-active = COND #( WHEN r_fg = 'X' THEN 0 ELSE 1 ).
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      WHEN 'ID2'.
        screen-active = COND #( WHEN r_bg = 'X' THEN 0 ELSE 1 ).
        MODIFY SCREEN.
    ENDCASE.
  ENDLOOP.


START-OF-SELECTION.

  LOOP AT s_var INTO DATA(wa_var).

    DATA: lt_seltab TYPE TABLE OF rsparams,
          ls_seltab LIKE LINE OF lt_seltab.

    LOOP AT s_budat.
      CLEAR: ls_seltab, lt_seltab.
      ls_seltab-selname = 'R_BUDAT'.          " Name of parameter on submitted program
      ls_seltab-kind    = 'S'.
      ls_seltab-sign    = s_budat-sign.
      ls_seltab-option  = s_budat-option.
      ls_seltab-low     = s_budat-low.
      ls_seltab-high    = s_budat-high.
      APPEND ls_seltab TO lt_seltab.
    ENDLOOP.

    CLEAR: lv_variant.
    lv_variant = wa_var-low.

    TRY.
        cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>set(
             EXPORTING
               display  = abap_false
               metadata = abap_false
               data     = abap_true ).

        " Submit the report with variant

        SUBMIT rkaep000
          USING SELECTION-SET lv_variant
                WITH SELECTION-TABLE lt_seltab
          "WITH BUDAT IN s_budat
          AND RETURN.

        " Get the internal table
        cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>get_data_ref(
          IMPORTING
            r_data = lt_output ).

        ASSIGN lt_output->* TO <lt_data>.

        "---------------mapping the data to the internal tables------
        LOOP AT <lt_data> ASSIGNING <ls_row>.

          IF ( ( r_fg = 'X' AND p_xlsx = 'X' ) OR ( r_bg = 'X' AND p_xlsx = 'X' ) ).
            " Assign each field from <ls_row> to wa_final_data
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BUKRS' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val1>).
            IF <val1> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-bukrs = <val1>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'REFBN' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val2>).
            IF <val2> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-refbn = <val2>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'KSTAR' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val3>).
            IF <val3> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-kstar = <val3>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'KOSTL' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val4>).
            IF <val4> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-kostl = <val4>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CEL_KTXT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val5>).
            IF <val5> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-cel_ktxt = <val5>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BLTXT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val6>).
            IF <val6> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-bltxt = <val6>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'MAT_TXT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val7>).
            IF <val7> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-mat_txt = <val7>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'SGTXT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val8>).
            IF <val8> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-sgtxt = <val8>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'EBELN' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val9>).
            IF <val9> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-ebeln = <val9>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BUDAT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val10>).
            IF <val10> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final-budat = <val10>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'WOVBTR' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val11>).
            IF <val11> IS ASSIGNED.
              CLEAR: lv_amount, lv_amount_str.
              lv_amount = <val11>.
              IF lv_amount < 0.
                lv_amount_str = |-{ abs( lv_amount ) }|.
              ELSE.
                lv_amount_str = |{ lv_amount }|.
              ENDIF.
              wa_final-wovbtr = lv_amount_str.
            ENDIF.
*            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CPUDT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val11>).
*            IF <val11> IS ASSIGNED.
*              wa_final-cpudt = <val11>.
*            ENDIF.
*            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'KWAER' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<val12>).
*            IF <val12> IS ASSIGNED.
*              wa_final-kwaer = <val12>.
*            ENDIF.
            APPEND wa_final TO lt_final.
            CLEAR wa_final.
          ENDIF.

          IF ( ( r_fg = 'X' AND p_pdf = 'X' ) OR ( r_bg = 'X' AND p_pdf = 'X' ) ).

            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BUKRS' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val1>.
            IF <val1> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-bukrs = <val1>.
            ENDIF.

            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'REFBN' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val2>.
            IF <val2> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-refbn = <val2>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'KSTAR' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val3>.
            IF <val3> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-kstar = <val3>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'KOSTL' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val4>.
            IF <val4> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-kostl = <val4>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CEL_KTXT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val5>.
            IF <val5> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-cel_ktxt = <val5>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BLTXT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val6>.
            IF <val6> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-bltxt = <val6>.
            ENDIF.
             ASSIGN COMPONENT 'MAT_TXT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val7>.
            IF <val7> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-mat_txt = <val7>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'SGTXT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val8>.
            IF <val8> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-sgtxt = <val8>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'EBELN' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val9>.
            IF <val9> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-ebeln = <val9>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BUDAT' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val10>.
            IF <val10> IS ASSIGNED.
              wa_final_pdf-budat = <val10>.
            ENDIF.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'WOVBTR' OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <val11>.
            IF <val11> IS ASSIGNED.
              CLEAR: lv_amount, lv_amount_str.
              lv_amount = <val11>.
              IF lv_amount < 0.
                lv_amount_str = |-{ abs( lv_amount ) }|.
              ELSE.
                lv_amount_str = |{ lv_amount }|.
              ENDIF.
              wa_final_pdf-wovbtr = lv_amount_str.
            ENDIF.

            APPEND wa_final_pdf TO lt_final_pdf.
            CLEAR wa_final_pdf.
          ENDIF.
        ENDLOOP.
*--------------logic for subtotals and totals for xls---------------------

        DATA: lt_final_data     TYPE TABLE OF ty_final,
              wa_final_data     TYPE ty_final,
              lt_final_data1    TYPE TABLE OF ty_final_pdf,
              wa_final_data1    TYPE ty_final_pdf,
              lv_prev_kostl     TYPE kostl,
              lv_prev_kstar     TYPE kstar,
              lv_prev_perio     TYPE perio,
              lv_subtotal       TYPE string,
              lv_subtotal_kstar TYPE p DECIMALS 2,
              lv_subtotal_kostl TYPE p DECIMALS 2,
              lv_subtotal_perio TYPE p DECIMALS 2,
              lv_grand_total    TYPE string.
        DATA: lv_formatted_subtotal    TYPE string,
              lv_formatted_grand_total TYPE string,
              lv_wovbtr_num            TYPE p DECIMALS 2.


        IF ( ( r_fg = 'X' AND p_xlsx = 'X' ) OR ( r_bg = 'X' AND p_xlsx = 'X' ) ).

          SORT lt_final BY kostl kstar .

          LOOP AT lt_final INTO wa_final.
            " Now convert to numeric
            CLEAR lv_wovbtr_num.
            lv_wovbtr_num = wa_final-wovbtr."lv_wkgbtr_raw.

            " Check for cost element change
            IF lv_prev_kstar IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_prev_kstar <> wa_final-kstar.
              PERFORM insert_subtotal USING lv_prev_kostl lv_prev_kstar lv_subtotal_kstar '*** Subtotal (Cost Element) ***'.          "lv_prev_perio
              CLEAR lv_subtotal_kstar.
            ENDIF.

            " Check for cost center change
            IF lv_prev_kostl IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_prev_kostl <> wa_final-kostl.
              PERFORM insert_subtotal USING lv_prev_kostl '' lv_subtotal_kostl '*** Subtotal (Cost Center) ***'.
              CLEAR lv_subtotal_kostl.
            ENDIF.

            " Check for period change
*            IF lv_prev_perio IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_prev_perio <> wa_final-perio.
*              PERFORM insert_subtotal USING '' '' lv_prev_perio lv_subtotal_perio '*** Subtotal (Period) ***'.
*              CLEAR lv_subtotal_perio.
*            ENDIF.

            " Accumulate subtotals
            lv_subtotal_kstar  = lv_subtotal_kstar  + lv_wovbtr_num.
            lv_subtotal_kostl  = lv_subtotal_kostl  + lv_wovbtr_num.
*            lv_subtotal_perio  = lv_subtotal_perio  + lv_wovbtr_num.
            lv_grand_total     = lv_grand_total     + lv_wovbtr_num.

            " Format wkgbtr back to string
            IF lv_wovbtr_num < 0.
              wa_final-wovbtr = |-{ abs( lv_wovbtr_num ) }|.
            ELSE.
              wa_final-wovbtr = |{ lv_wovbtr_num }|.
            ENDIF.
            APPEND wa_final TO lt_final_data.

            " Update previous values
            lv_prev_kostl = wa_final-kostl.
            lv_prev_kstar = wa_final-kstar.
*            lv_prev_perio = wa_final-perio.
          ENDLOOP.

          " Final subtotals
          PERFORM insert_subtotal USING lv_prev_kostl lv_prev_kstar lv_subtotal_kstar '*** Subtotal (Cost Element) ***'.
          PERFORM insert_subtotal USING lv_prev_kostl '' lv_subtotal_kostl '*** Subtotal (Cost Center) ***'.
*          PERFORM insert_subtotal USING '' '' lv_prev_perio lv_subtotal_perio '*** Subtotal (Period) ***'.

          " Grand total
          CLEAR wa_final_data.
          wa_final_data-sgtxt = '*** Grand Total ***'.
          IF lv_grand_total < 0.
            lv_formatted_grand_total = |-{ abs( lv_grand_total ) }|.
          ELSE.
            lv_formatted_grand_total = |{ lv_grand_total }|.
          ENDIF.
          wa_final_data-wovbtr = lv_formatted_grand_total.
          APPEND wa_final_data TO lt_final_data.

          CLEAR: wa_final_data,lv_formatted_subtotal,lv_formatted_grand_total,lv_grand_total.
          CLEAR: lv_prev_kostl, lv_prev_kstar, lv_prev_perio,lv_subtotal_kstar, lv_subtotal_kostl, lv_subtotal_perio.

        ENDIF.

        "for Pdf logic
        IF ( ( r_fg = 'X' AND p_pdf = 'X' ) OR ( r_bg = 'X' AND p_pdf = 'X' ) ).

          SORT lt_final_pdf BY kostl kstar.

          LOOP AT lt_final_pdf INTO wa_final_pdf.
            " Now convert to numeric
            CLEAR lv_wovbtr_num.
            lv_wovbtr_num = wa_final_pdf-wovbtr."lv_wkgbtr_raw.

            " Check for cost element change
            IF lv_prev_kstar IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_prev_kstar <> wa_final_pdf-kstar.
              PERFORM insert_subtotal_pdf USING lv_prev_kostl lv_prev_kstar lv_subtotal_kstar '*** Subtotal (Cost Element) ***'.
              CLEAR lv_subtotal_kstar.
            ENDIF.

            " Check for cost center change
            IF lv_prev_kostl IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_prev_kostl <> wa_final_pdf-kostl.
              PERFORM insert_subtotal_pdf USING lv_prev_kostl '' lv_subtotal_kostl '*** Subtotal (Cost Center) ***'.
              CLEAR lv_subtotal_kostl.
            ENDIF.

            " Check for period change
*            IF lv_prev_perio IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_prev_perio <> wa_final_pdf-perio.
*              PERFORM insert_subtotal_pdf USING '' '' lv_prev_perio lv_subtotal_perio '*** Subtotal (Period) ***'.
*              CLEAR lv_subtotal_perio.
*            ENDIF.

            " Accumulate subtotals
            lv_subtotal_kstar  = lv_subtotal_kstar  + lv_wovbtr_num.
            lv_subtotal_kostl  = lv_subtotal_kostl  + lv_wovbtr_num.
           " lv_subtotal_perio  = lv_subtotal_perio  + lv_wovbtr_num.
            lv_grand_total     = lv_grand_total     + lv_wovbtr_num.

            " Format wkgbtr back to string
            IF lv_wovbtr_num < 0.
              wa_final_pdf-wovbtr = |-{ abs( lv_wovbtr_num ) }|.
            ELSE.
              wa_final_pdf-wovbtr = |{ lv_wovbtr_num }|.
            ENDIF.

            APPEND wa_final_pdf TO lt_final_data1.

            " Update previous values
            lv_prev_kostl = wa_final_pdf-kostl.
            lv_prev_kstar = wa_final_pdf-kstar.
           " lv_prev_perio = wa_final_pdf-perio.
          ENDLOOP.

          " Final subtotals
          PERFORM insert_subtotal_pdf USING lv_prev_kostl lv_prev_kstar lv_subtotal_kstar '*** Subtotal (Cost Element) ***'.
          PERFORM insert_subtotal_pdf USING lv_prev_kostl '' lv_subtotal_kostl '*** Subtotal (Cost Center) ***'.
          "PERFORM insert_subtotal_pdf USING '' '' lv_prev_perio lv_subtotal_perio '*** Subtotal (Period) ***'.

          " Grand total
          CLEAR wa_final_data1.
          wa_final_data1-sgtxt = '*** Grand Total ***'.
          IF lv_grand_total < 0.
            lv_formatted_grand_total = |-{ abs( lv_grand_total ) }|.
          ELSE.
            lv_formatted_grand_total = |{ lv_grand_total }|.
          ENDIF.
          wa_final_data1-wovbtr = lv_formatted_grand_total.
          APPEND wa_final_data1 TO lt_final_data1.

          CLEAR: wa_final_data1,lv_formatted_subtotal,lv_formatted_grand_total,lv_grand_total.
          CLEAR: lv_prev_kostl, lv_prev_kstar, lv_prev_perio,lv_subtotal_kstar, lv_subtotal_kostl. "lv_subtotal_perio.

        ENDIF.

*------- logic to get the path and filenames-------------------------
        DATA: lv_file_pdf TYPE so_obj_des,
              lv_file_xls TYPE so_obj_des,
              lv_subject  TYPE so_obj_des,
              lv_filename TYPE so_obj_des.

        CLEAR:lv_file_pdf,lv_subject,lv_file_xls,lv_path_xlsx,lv_path_pdf,lv_filename.

        DATA(lv_date_us) = |{ sy-datum+4(2) }-{ sy-datum+6(2) }-{ sy-datum+0(4) }|.

        lv_filename = |{ lv_variant }_{ lv_date_us }_{ sy-uzeit+0(2) }-{ sy-uzeit+2(2) }-{ sy-uzeit+4(2) }|.
        lv_path_xlsx = p_path && '\' && lv_filename && '.xlsx'.
        lv_path_pdf =  p_path && '\' && lv_filename && '.PDF'.
        lv_file_pdf = |{ lv_variant }_{ lv_date_us }_| & |{ sy-uzeit+0(2) }-{ sy-uzeit+2(2) }-{ sy-uzeit+4(2) }.pdf|.
        lv_file_xls = |{ lv_variant }_{ lv_date_us }_| & |{ sy-uzeit+0(2) }-{ sy-uzeit+2(2) }-{ sy-uzeit+4(2) }.xls|.
        CONCATENATE lv_variant '-Report' INTO lv_subject SEPARATED BY space.

        DATA: lv_action_done TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false.

        " Convert to xlsx
        IF sy-batch <> 'X'.

          IF r_fg = 'X' AND p_xlsx = 'X'.
            PERFORM download_xlsx TABLES lt_final_data
                                  USING lv_path_xlsx.
            lv_action_done = abap_true.
          ENDIF.

          IF r_fg = 'X' AND p_pdf = 'X'.
            PERFORM download_pdf TABLES lt_final_data1
                                 USING lv_path_pdf.
            lv_action_done = abap_true.
          ENDIF.

        ELSE.

          IF r_bg = 'X' AND p_xlsx = 'X' AND p_pdf = 'X'.
            PERFORM sendemail_combined TABLES lt_final_data1 lt_final_data
                                       USING lv_subject lv_file_xls lv_file_pdf p_email.
            lv_action_done = abap_true.

          ELSEIF r_bg = 'X' AND p_xlsx = 'X'.
            PERFORM sendemail_xls TABLES lt_final_data
                                  USING lv_subject lv_file_xls.
            lv_action_done = abap_true.

          ELSEIF r_bg = 'X' AND p_pdf = 'X'.
            PERFORM sendemail_pdf TABLES lt_final_data1
                                  USING lv_subject lv_file_pdf.
            lv_action_done = abap_true.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

        IF lv_action_done = abap_false.
          MESSAGE 'Please select the format to download' TYPE 'I'.
        ENDIF.

      CATCH cx_salv_bs_sc_runtime_info INTO DATA(lx_info).
        WRITE: / 'Error capturing ALV for variant:', lv_variant.

        cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>clear_all( ).
    ENDTRY.

    CLEAR: lt_output,lt_final,lt_final_pdf,wa_var,lt_final_data1, lt_final_data.

  ENDLOOP.
**&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
**& Form download_xlsx
**&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
**& text
**&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
**&      --> LV_PATH_XLSX
**&      --> LT_FINAL
**&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM download_xlsx  TABLES p_lt_final_data LIKE lt_final_data
                    USING  p_lv_path_xlsx.

  DATA: lt_final_xlsx TYPE TABLE OF ty_final.

  CLEAR lt_final_xlsx.
  lt_final_xlsx[] = p_lt_final_data[].

  DATA: lr_table     TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,          " Basis Class for Simple Tables
        lr_functions TYPE REF TO cl_salv_functions_list, " Generic and User-Defined Functions in List-Type Tables
        lr_layout    TYPE REF TO cl_salv_layout,         " Settings for Layout
        lv_xml_type  TYPE salv_bs_constant,
        lv_xml       TYPE xstring,
        lt_srctab    TYPE solix_tab,
        lt_len       TYPE i,                             " Len of type Integers
        ls_key       TYPE salv_s_layout_key,             " Layout Key
        lr_content   TYPE REF TO cl_salv_form_element.


  TRY.
      cl_salv_table=>factory(
        IMPORTING
          r_salv_table = lr_table
        CHANGING
          t_table      = lt_final_xlsx[] ).
    CATCH cx_salv_msg.                                  "#EC NO_HANDLER
  ENDTRY.

  " Set custom column headers

  DATA: lv_colname TYPE lvc_fname.

  lv_colname = 'WOVBTR'. " or any other field name from your structure

  DATA:lo_columns TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns_table,
       lo_column  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column.

  lo_columns = lr_table->get_columns( ).

  TRY.
      lo_column = lo_columns->get_column( lv_colname ).
      lo_column->set_long_text( 'Vbl. value/Obj. curr' ).
      lo_column->set_medium_text( 'Vbl. value/Obj. curr' ).
      lo_column->set_short_text( 'Vbl. Curr' ).
    CATCH cx_salv_not_found.
      " Handle missing column gracefully
  ENDTRY.

  lr_functions = lr_table->get_functions( ).
  lr_functions->set_all( abap_true ).
  lr_layout = lr_table->get_layout( ).
  ls_key-report = sy-repid.
  lr_layout->set_key( ls_key ).
  lr_layout->set_save_restriction( if_salv_c_layout=>restrict_user_independant ).
  lr_table->set_top_of_list( lr_content ).
  lr_table->set_end_of_list( lr_content ).
**  gr_table->display( ).
  lv_xml_type =  if_salv_bs_xml=>c_type_xlsx. "if_salv_bs_xml=>c_type_mhtml.
  lv_xml      = lr_table->to_xml( xml_type = lv_xml_type ).

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer        = lv_xml
*     APPEND_TO_TABLE       = ' '
    IMPORTING
      output_length = lt_len
    TABLES
      binary_tab    = lt_srctab.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download
    EXPORTING
      bin_filesize            = lt_len
      filename                = p_lv_path_xlsx
      filetype                = 'BIN'
    CHANGING
      data_tab                = lt_srctab
    EXCEPTIONS
      file_write_error        = 1
      no_batch                = 2
      gui_refuse_filetransfer = 3
      invalid_type            = 4
      dp_out_of_memory        = 16
      disk_full               = 17
      dp_timeout              = 18
      file_not_found          = 19
      dataprovider_exception  = 20
      control_flush_error     = 21
      not_supported_by_gui    = 22
      error_no_gui            = 23
      OTHERS                  = 24.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
               WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
  ELSE. " ELSE -> IF sy-subrc <> 0
    MESSAGE 'Excel file downloaded!' TYPE 'S'.
  ENDIF.
  CLEAR: lt_srctab,lt_len,lv_xml,lr_table,lr_functions,lr_layout,ls_key,lv_xml_type.

ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form download_pdf
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      --> LV_PATH_PDF
*&      --> LT_FINAL
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM download_pdf  TABLES p_lt_final_data1 LIKE lt_final_data1
                   USING  p_lv_path_pdf.
  DATA: lt_final_pdf TYPE TABLE OF ty_final_pdf.

  CLEAR lt_final_pdf.
  lt_final_pdf[] = p_lt_final_data1[].

  DATA: lo_pdf    TYPE REF TO cl_apoc_pdf_generator,
        rv_bin    TYPE xstring,
        lt_binary TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF x255,
        lv_len    TYPE i.

  lo_pdf = NEW cl_apoc_pdf_generator( ).
  lo_pdf->create_pdf(
    EXPORTING
      input             = lt_final_pdf
    RECEIVING
      pdf_binary_output = rv_bin ).

  " Convert xstring to binary table
  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer        = rv_bin
    IMPORTING
      output_length = lv_len
    TABLES
      binary_tab    = lt_binary.

  " Download the file
  CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      bin_filesize = lv_len
      filename     = p_lv_path_pdf
      filetype     = 'BIN'
    TABLES
      data_tab     = lt_binary.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
               WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
  ELSE. " ELSE -> IF sy-subrc <> 0
    MESSAGE 'PDF file downloaded!' TYPE 'S'.
  ENDIF.
  CLEAR:lv_len,rv_bin,lt_binary,lo_pdf.

ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form sendemail_xls
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      --> LT_FINAL
*&      --> LV_SUBJECT
*&      --> LV_FILE_XLS
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM sendemail_xls TABLES p_lt_final_data LIKE lt_final_data
                   USING  p_lv_subject
                          p_lv_file_xls.
  DATA :
    lv_data_string TYPE string,
    lv_xstring     TYPE xstring,
    lv_count       TYPE string VALUE '01',
    lv_data        TYPE string.

  DATA:
    lo_document  TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs,
    lt_mailtxt   TYPE bcsy_text,
    lo_doc       TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs,
    lo_send_req  TYPE REF TO cl_bcs,
    lo_recipient TYPE REF TO if_recipient_bcs.

  DATA: lit_binary_content TYPE solix_tab.
  DATA: it_mail  TYPE TABLE OF ty_final,
        wa_mail  TYPE ty_final,
        wa_final TYPE ty_final.

** Mail body
  APPEND 'Dear User,' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND ' ' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'Please find attached report.' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND ' ' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'Regards,' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'SAP System' TO lt_mailtxt.

  CLEAR : lv_data.
  CONCATENATE TEXT-003
              TEXT-004
              TEXT-005
              TEXT-006
              TEXT-007
              TEXT-008
              TEXT-009
              TEXT-010
              TEXT-011
              TEXT-012
              TEXT-013
*              TEXT-014
              INTO lv_data SEPARATED BY cl_abap_char_utilities=>horizontal_tab.

  CONCATENATE  lv_data '' INTO lv_data_string .

  LOOP AT p_lt_final_data INTO wa_final.
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING wa_final TO wa_mail.
    CONDENSE:     wa_mail-bukrs,
                  wa_mail-refbn,
                  wa_mail-kstar,
                  wa_mail-kostl,
                  wa_mail-cel_ktxt,
                  wa_mail-bltxt,
                  wa_mail-mat_txt,
                  wa_mail-sgtxt,
                  wa_mail-ebeln,
                  wa_mail-budat,
                  wa_mail-wovbtr.
*                  wa_mail-cpudt,
*                  wa_mail-kwaer.

    CONCATENATE   wa_mail-bukrs
                  wa_mail-refbn
                  wa_mail-kstar
                  wa_mail-kostl
                  wa_mail-cel_ktxt
                  wa_mail-bltxt
                  wa_mail-mat_txt
                  wa_mail-sgtxt
                  wa_mail-ebeln
                  wa_mail-budat
*                  wa_mail-perio
                  wa_mail-wovbtr
    INTO lv_data SEPARATED BY cl_abap_char_utilities=>horizontal_tab.
    CONDENSE lv_data.
    CONCATENATE  lv_data_string lv_data    INTO lv_data_string  SEPARATED BY cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline.

  ENDLOOP.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_STRING_TO_XSTRING'
    EXPORTING
      text   = lv_data_string
*     MIMETYPE       = ' '
*     ENCODING       =
    IMPORTING
      buffer = lv_xstring
    EXCEPTIONS
      failed = 1
      OTHERS = 2.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    IF sy-subrc = 1 .
      MESSAGE e000(38) WITH  'Failure in conversion (SCMS_STRING_TO_XSTRING)'(111).
    ELSEIF sy-subrc = 2 .
      MESSAGE e000(38) WITH  'Other Error (SCMS_STRING_TO_XSTRING)'(112).
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.


  CLEAR : lit_binary_content.
**Xstring to binary
  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer     = lv_xstring
    TABLES
      binary_tab = lit_binary_content.
*
* Create document
  lo_doc = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
    i_type    = 'RAW'
    i_text    = lt_mailtxt
    i_subject = p_lv_subject ).

* Attach XLSX
  lo_doc->add_attachment(
    i_attachment_type    = 'XLS'
    i_attachment_subject = p_lv_file_xls
    i_att_content_hex    = lit_binary_content ).

* Send request
  lo_send_req = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
  lo_send_req->set_document( lo_doc ).

  lo_recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( p_email ).
  lo_send_req->add_recipient( lo_recipient ).

  TRY.
      lo_send_req->send( i_with_error_screen = abap_false ).
      COMMIT WORK.
      MESSAGE 'Email sent successfully' TYPE 'S'.
    CATCH cx_bcs INTO DATA(lx_bcs).
      MESSAGE lx_bcs->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.

  CLEAR: lx_bcs,lt_mailtxt,lv_xstring,lv_data_string.
  FREE: lo_doc,lo_send_req,lo_recipient.

ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form sendemail_pdf
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      --> LT_FINAL
*&      --> LV_SUBJECT
*&      --> LV_FILE_PDF
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM sendemail_pdf  TABLES   p_lt_final_data1 LIKE lt_final_data1
                    USING    p_lv_subject
                             p_lv_file_pdf.


  DATA: lo_pdf TYPE REF TO cl_apoc_pdf_generator,
        rv_bin TYPE xstring,
        lv_len TYPE i.
  DATA: it_mail TYPE TABLE OF ty_final_pdf.

  it_mail[] = p_lt_final_data1[].

  DATA: lt_pdf_binary TYPE solix_tab,
*      lv_size         TYPE so_obj_len,
*      lo_send_request TYPE REF TO cl_bcs,
        lo_document   TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs,
        lt_mailtxt    TYPE bcsy_text,
        lo_doc        TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs,
        lo_send_req   TYPE REF TO cl_bcs,
        lo_recipient  TYPE REF TO if_recipient_bcs.

  lo_pdf = NEW cl_apoc_pdf_generator( ).
  lo_pdf->create_pdf(
    EXPORTING
      input             = it_mail
    RECEIVING
      pdf_binary_output = rv_bin
  ).

  " Convert xstring to binary table
  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer        = rv_bin
    IMPORTING
      output_length = lv_len
    TABLES
      binary_tab    = lt_pdf_binary.


  APPEND 'Dear User,' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND ' ' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'Please find attached report.' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND ' ' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'Regards,' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'SAP System' TO lt_mailtxt.

** Create document
  lo_doc = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
    i_type    = 'RAW'
    i_text    = lt_mailtxt
    i_subject = p_lv_subject ).

* Attach XLSX
  lo_doc->add_attachment(
    i_attachment_type    = 'PDF'
    i_attachment_subject = p_lv_file_pdf
    i_att_content_hex    = lt_pdf_binary ).

* Send request
  lo_send_req = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
  lo_send_req->set_document( lo_doc ).

  lo_recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( p_email ).
  lo_send_req->add_recipient( lo_recipient ).

  TRY.
      lo_send_req->send( i_with_error_screen = abap_false ).
      COMMIT WORK.
      MESSAGE 'Email sent successfully' TYPE 'S'.
    CATCH cx_bcs INTO DATA(lx_bcs).
      MESSAGE lx_bcs->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.
  CLEAR: lt_mailtxt,lt_pdf_binary,lx_bcs,rv_bin,lv_len,lo_pdf.
  FREE: lo_doc,lo_send_req,lo_recipient.

ENDFORM.

FORM get_file_path.
  DATA: lv_folder TYPE string.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>directory_browse
    CHANGING
      selected_folder      = lv_folder
    EXCEPTIONS
      cntl_error           = 1
      error_no_gui         = 2
      not_supported_by_gui = 3
      OTHERS               = 4.

  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    p_path = lv_folder.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.
FORM sendemail_combined TABLES p_lt_final_data1 LIKE lt_final_data1
                                p_lt_final_data LIKE lt_final_data
                         USING  p_lv_subject
                                p_lv_file_xls
                                p_lv_file_pdf
                                p_email.

  DATA: lv_data_string     TYPE string,
        lv_xstring         TYPE xstring,
        lv_data            TYPE string,
        lit_binary_content TYPE solix_tab,
        lt_pdf_binary      TYPE solix_tab,
        rv_bin             TYPE xstring,
        lv_len             TYPE i.

  DATA: lo_doc       TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs,
        lo_send_req  TYPE REF TO cl_bcs,
        lo_recipient TYPE REF TO if_recipient_bcs,
        lt_mailtxt   TYPE bcsy_text,
        lo_pdf       TYPE REF TO cl_apoc_pdf_generator.

  DATA: it_final TYPE TABLE OF ty_final_pdf,
        it_mail  TYPE TABLE OF ty_final,
        wa_mail  TYPE ty_final.

  it_final[] = p_lt_final_data1[].

  " Mail body
  APPEND 'Dear User,' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND ' ' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'Please find attached reports of the varient.' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND ' ' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'Regards,' TO lt_mailtxt.
  APPEND 'SAP System' TO lt_mailtxt.

  " Create document with mail body
  lo_doc = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
    i_type    = 'RAW'
    i_text    = lt_mailtxt
    i_subject = p_lv_subject ).

  " Generate XLS content
  CLEAR : lv_data.
  CONCATENATE TEXT-003
              TEXT-004
              TEXT-005
              TEXT-006
              TEXT-007
              TEXT-008
              TEXT-009
              TEXT-010
              TEXT-011
              TEXT-012
              TEXT-013
*              TEXT-014
              INTO lv_data SEPARATED BY cl_abap_char_utilities=>horizontal_tab.

  CONCATENATE  lv_data '' INTO lv_data_string .

  LOOP AT p_lt_final_data INTO wa_final.
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING wa_final TO wa_mail.
    CONDENSE:     wa_mail-bukrs,
                  wa_mail-refbn,
                  wa_mail-kstar,
                  wa_mail-kostl,
                  wa_mail-cel_ktxt,
                  wa_mail-bltxt,
                  wa_mail-mat_txt,
                  wa_mail-sgtxt,
                  wa_mail-ebeln,
                  wa_mail-budat,
                  wa_mail-wovbtr.
*                  wa_mail-cpudt,
*                  wa_mail-kwaer.

    CONCATENATE   wa_mail-bukrs
                  wa_mail-refbn
                  wa_mail-kstar
                  wa_mail-kostl
                  wa_mail-cel_ktxt
                  wa_mail-bltxt
                  wa_mail-mat_txt
                  wa_mail-sgtxt
                  wa_mail-ebeln
                  wa_mail-budat
*                  wa_mail-perio
                  wa_mail-wovbtr
*                  wa_mail-cpudt
*                  wa_mail-kwaer
    INTO lv_data SEPARATED BY cl_abap_char_utilities=>horizontal_tab.
    CONDENSE lv_data.
    CONCATENATE  lv_data_string lv_data    INTO lv_data_string  SEPARATED BY cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline.

  ENDLOOP.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_STRING_TO_XSTRING'
    EXPORTING
      text   = lv_data_string
    IMPORTING
      buffer = lv_xstring.

  CLEAR : lit_binary_content.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer     = lv_xstring
    TABLES
      binary_tab = lit_binary_content.

  " Attach XLS
  lo_doc->add_attachment(
    i_attachment_type    = 'XLS'
    i_attachment_subject = p_lv_file_xls
    i_att_content_hex    = lit_binary_content ).

  " Generate PDF content
  lo_pdf = NEW cl_apoc_pdf_generator( ).
  lo_pdf->create_pdf(
    EXPORTING input = it_final
    RECEIVING pdf_binary_output = rv_bin ).

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer        = rv_bin
    IMPORTING
      output_length = lv_len
    TABLES
      binary_tab    = lt_pdf_binary.

  " Attach PDF
  lo_doc->add_attachment(
    i_attachment_type    = 'PDF'
    i_attachment_subject = p_lv_file_pdf
    i_att_content_hex    = lt_pdf_binary ).

  " Send email
  lo_send_req = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
  lo_send_req->set_document( lo_doc ).
  lo_recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( p_email ).
  lo_send_req->add_recipient( lo_recipient ).

  TRY.
      lo_send_req->send( i_with_error_screen = abap_false ).
      COMMIT WORK.
      MESSAGE 'Email sent successfully with PDF and XLS attachments' TYPE 'S'.
    CATCH cx_bcs INTO DATA(lx_bcs).
      MESSAGE lx_bcs->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.

  CLEAR: it_mail,wa_mail,it_final,lt_pdf_binary,rv_bin,lv_len,lo_pdf.
  CLEAR: lx_bcs,lt_mailtxt,lv_xstring,lv_data_string.
  FREE: lo_doc,lo_send_req,lo_recipient.
ENDFORM.

FORM insert_subtotal USING p_kostl p_kstar  p_amount p_label.


  wa_final_data-kostl = p_kostl.
  wa_final_data-kstar = p_kstar.
 " wa_final_data-perio = p_perio.
  wa_final_data-sgtxt = p_label.

  IF p_amount < 0.
    wa_final_data-wovbtr = |-{ abs( p_amount ) }|.
  ELSE.
    wa_final_data-wovbtr = |{ p_amount }|.
  ENDIF.

  APPEND wa_final_data TO lt_final_data.
    CLEAR wa_final_data.
ENDFORM.

FORM insert_subtotal_pdf USING p_kostl p_kstar p_amount p_label.


  wa_final_data1-kostl = p_kostl.
  wa_final_data1-kstar = p_kstar.
 " wa_final_data1-perio = p_perio.
  wa_final_data1-sgtxt = p_label.

  IF p_amount < 0.
    wa_final_data1-wovbtr = |-{ abs( p_amount ) }|.
  ELSE.
    wa_final_data1-wovbtr = |{ p_amount }|.
  ENDIF.

  APPEND wa_final_data1 TO lt_final_data1.
  CLEAR wa_final_data1.
ENDFORM.

Regards

Pankaj

entry.title: Re: Notas Fiscais não processadas após execução do job ED...
entry.content: Hi Marcos, Buffer time refers to intentionally delaying the upper limit of the retrieval window to avoid any issues. For e.g.if the current time is 14:00, instead of retrieving documents up to 14:00, you retrieve only up to 13:50—hence, a 10-minute buffer. I hope its clear now.

entry.title: Re: HR Trigger functionality is not automatically removin...
entry.content: For role removal try - SPRO → Governance, Risk and Compliance → Access Control → Role Management → Maintain Role Removal Criteria. you define criteria to select which roles should be automatically removed when an HR Trigger, for e.g.Remove (in your case). Here, you can define rules based on - Role Type (Business / Technical), Assignment Type (Direct / Indirect), User Group, Role Name pattern, etc.

entry.title: Re: Adjusting Reconciliation Account in Asset Accounting...

entry.title: The Future of Planning in Excel: The SAP Analytics Cloud Add-in as a Strategic Frontend – Part 1/3
entry.content:
Introduction

For many business users in finance and controlling, Microsoft Excel is more than just software — it’s the daily tool for analysis, reporting, and, above all, corporate planning. Its flexibility and familiar environment are unbeatable. But how do you combine this flexibility with the power, security, and scalability of a central planning platform like the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)?

The answer is the SAP Analytics Cloud Add-in for Microsoft Office. This modern add-in is positioned as the strategic successor to the proven SAP Analysis for Office, bridging the gap between the familiar world of Excel and the powerful in-memory planning engine of SAC.

A Quick Comparison: Analysis for Office vs. SAC Add-in

For anyone who has worked with SAP data in Excel for years, SAP Analysis for Office (AfO) is an old friend. It’s a robust and feature-rich tool, primarily designed for analyzing data from SAP BW and S/4HANA. It is based on traditional COM add-in technology and is heavily focused on querying BEx Queries or CDS Views.

The new SAC Add-in, on the other hand, was built from the ground up as a native frontend for the SAP Analytics Cloud. Here are the key differences and advantages at a glance:

  • Architecture: The SAC add-in is a modern Microsoft Office 365 add-in (based on WebView2). This allows for centralized, cloud-based deployment and management directly from the Microsoft Admin Center—without cumbersome client installations.
  • Connectivity: While AfO connects directly to backend systems like SAP BW, the primary and sole anchor point for the SAC add-in is your SAC tenant. All data, models, and permissions are sourced centrally from SAC.
  • Focus on Planning: The add-in is optimized for SAC planning models. Features like data entry, running data actions, version management, and using planning features are seamlessly integrated. You work in Excel but leverage the full power of the SAC planning engine in the background.
  • Future Strategy: SAP is clearly positioning the SAC add-in as the strategic tool for Excel integration in the cloud world.

The Architecture: Native Planning and „Seamless Integration“ into Datasphere

The true strength of the new approach lies in its seamless architecture. Instead of keeping planning data in separate silos, SAC allows for direct integration with SAP Datasphere.

This concept ensures that planning data entered in Excel via the SAC add-in can be written directly into the underlying structures of SAP Datasphere. The data flow remains consistent and is always controlled by SAC.

Architecture Diagram: Planning with the SAC Add-in
Architecture

Building an Interactive Planning Sheet

A simple data table is just the beginning, but the true strength of the add-in lies in the combination of SAC data and the flexibility of Excel. Our goal is not just to display data, but to design an interactive planning input screen.

1. Define a Clear Layout:

Using the „Builder“ panel, drag the required dimensions into the rows and columns.

 

2. Use Excel Formulas for Ad-Hoc Analysis:

This is the key advantage: you can use native Excel formulas right next to the data loaded from SAC. For example, add a sum column and calculate it with a simple formula like =SUM(C7:E7). This cell updates dynamically as soon as new plan data is entered or changed.

 

3. Add Data Action Triggers:

Currently the excel add in supports triggering Data Actions directly (Multi Actions are planned for Q4 2025).

 

 

That’s it for the first part of this blog series, stay tuned for more advanced/technical content in the next blog post.

entry.title: Re: Official Documentation on Enabling Self-Use Users wit...
entry.content:
Hello @Melania2 

Thank you for your question.

Self-Use Users with the 30:1 FUE ratio can be enabled for basic operational tasks like

  • Purchase Requisition creation (App F2229)
  • Purchase Requisition approval
  • Goods Receipt posting

This is intended to support internal testing and familiarisation with key processes without consuming full FUE licences for each user.

Official Documentation and References:

  • Types of users for FUE - How to determine what each user type can do in SAP S/4HANA Cloud systems?
  • SAP Note 2655857 – Starter System: Restrictions and Usage Guidelines
  • Understanding S/4HANA Cloud Licensing & FUE Optimization

How to Enable it

Typically, Self-Use Users are configured via the Maintain Business Users and Maintain Business Roles apps, assigning roles like mentioned below

• SAP_BR_EMPLOYEE_PROCUREMENT for Purchase Requisitions
• SAP_BR_MANAGER or similar for approvals
• SAP_BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST for Goods Receipt

It's important to note that the Starter System has limitations in data persistence, integration scenarios, and certain extensibility options may be restricted compared to productive systems.

Let me know if you need further assistance

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Welcome to Your Integration Home: A Tour of SAP Integration Suite Tenant
entry.content:
Introduction:

Hey everyone! Do you remember the feeling of opening a new software for the first time? A mix of excitement and “where do I even click?” That’s perfectly normal, especially when you’re using a powerful platform like SAP Integration Suite.

This blog post is your ‘survival guide’ to the SAP Integration Suite Tenant. We'll focus on the essential areas you'll interact with daily, ensuring you don't get lost on your integration journey.

 

Section 1: How to Access Your Integration Home

  • Login / Sign up into the SAP BTP Cockpit Trial Account - https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/trial.html

SAP BTP Cockpit is the central point for all the SAP BTP Services.

  • After successful Login you’ll land into this page.

 

Click on ‘Go To Your Trial Account’.

 

  • Navigate to the Subaccount where your SAP Integration Suite service is provisioned.

 

  • Access the Integration Suite Service: In the left-hand navigation, look for "Services" and then "Instances and Subscriptions." Find "Integration Suite" and click on the "Go to Application" button.

 

Section 2: Main Section of the Tenant – An Overview

Once inside the Integration Suite, you’ll notice a navigation section on the left side which will guide you through the various functionalities. If you can’t see the navigation on the left side then first add all the capabilities into the Integration Suite. As you can see in the below image all the capabilities are added to the Integration Suite. Once all the capabilities are added logout and again login into the Integration Suite, navigation menu will be there.

These functionalities are the main “rooms” in our integration house:

  • Home: Your landing page with an overview, recent activities and quick links.

 

  • Design: Where you build and configure all your integration content.

 

  • Monitor: Once your integrations are live, this is where you keep an eye on them. Whether they are successfully deployed or not. This is the place where you can trace the log configuration of an integration flows.

 

  • Discover: This section is a rich repository of pre-packaged integration content provided by the SAP and its partners.

 

  • Settings: This section is more of administration use but developers also use it to manage users, roles, and most importantly, to activate and configure the various capabilities within Integration Suite like API Management, Open Connectors, etc.

 

Section 3: A Developer’s perspective on Each Section

Let’s focus on what you, as an integration developer, will be using in these sections:

      1. Design: This is where you’ll configure your integration scenarios. In this section, you’ll find:

  • Packages: Keep your integration content organized. Think of them as folders for your projects.

 

  • Integration Flows: This is the core where you’ll open the iFlow editor to graphically design your integration logic, drag-and-drop components, configure adapters, and define message processing steps.

            iFlow Editor –

 

  • Message Mappings & Value Mappings: Crucial for transforming data between different formats (e.g., converting an XML structure from one system to a JSON structure for another).

            Message Mapping Editor –

      2. Monitor: Once your iFlows are deployed, this section will become your best friend.

  • Message Processing: This is your primary message monitoring dashboard. Here, you can see every message that passes through your deployed iFlows. You’ll check their status (successful, failed, pending), view detailed logs, and inspect payloads. You can also activate tracing for debugging the iFlows in more efficient manner.

  • Integration Flows: This will provide a quick overview of your deployed iFlows, their runtime status (Started, Stopped), and the last processing time.

        3. Discover: Look for pre-built integration packages for common scenarios (e.g., integrating S/4HANA with Salesforce).  Using these packages will save a lot of your work.

      4. Settings:

  • Enabling Capabilities: Turn on additional features like API Management or Open Connectors if you require them in your scenario/project.
  • Reviewing Integrations: See which services are active and configured.

 

Conclusion:

Getting comfortable with the SAP BTP Integration Suite tenant is the first big step in your integration development journey on SAP BTP. By understanding the purpose of the functionalities of Integration Suite, you’re equipped to navigate efficiently, build robust integrations, and troubleshoot effectively.

Take some time to explore your own tenant. Click through each section, open some sample content in ‘Discover’, and get a feel for the environment. The more you explore, the more comfortable you’ll become.

entry.title: Replace All Occurrences in Multiple Fields of an Internal Table
entry.content:
This blog provides a smart way to clean special characters like @, #, !, and , from internal table data.
It helps when exporting data to CSV files, especially for AL11 directory usage.
Instead of writing code for each field, we clean all fields in one loop using field symbols.
RTTI (Runtime Type Info) is used to make it dynamic for any table.
This makes your exported data neat, clean, and ready for use.

Before Replacing


Code :

REPORT ZREPLACE_INTERNAL_TABLE.

"Define structure TY_DATA for sample person data
TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_DATA,
         NAME     TYPE STRING,
         CITY     TYPE STRING,
         EMAIL    TYPE STRING,
         ADDRESS  TYPE STRING,
         COMMENTS TYPE STRING,
       END OF TY_DATA.

"Internal table and work area to hold the data
DATA: LT_DATA TYPE TABLE OF TY_DATA,
      LS_DATA TYPE TY_DATA.

"Reference for SALV display
DATA: LT_FACTORTY  TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_TABLE.

"Sample data containing unwanted characters: @, #, !, ,
LT_DATA = VALUE #(
  ( name = 'Mani@ Yadav!' city = 'Hydera#bad' email = 'mani@yadav@example.com' address = 'Road No, 1, Banjara Hills' comments = 'Good@ Person!' )
  ( name = 'Anil Kumar' city = 'Mum#bai' email = 'anil.kumar@exa!mple.com' address = 'Apt@ 123, Green Society' comments = 'Very# Good, Employee' )
  ( name = 'Ravi Teja!' city = 'Hyder@abad' email = 'ravi.teja#@example.com' address = 'Street 45, Jubilee@ Hills' comments = 'Nice, and Reliable!' )
  ( name = 'Sushil Kumar' city = 'Chen@nai' email = 'sushil@kumar@example.com' address = 'Block 7, Ap@artment 3' comments = 'Great, Wo!rker' )
  ( name = 'Kavita@ Lal' city = 'Pune, City' email = 'kavita.lal#@example.com' address = 'MG Road@, Sector 2!' comments = 'Smart and@ Intellig!ent' )
  ( name = 'Arjun Mehta' city = 'Kolkata' email = 'arjun!mehta@example.com' address = 'Sector 9@, Block B' comments = 'Honest@ and Helpful!' )
  ( name = 'Deepa Agarwal#' city = 'Jai!pur' email = 'deepa@agarwal@example.com' address = 'Colony 5@, Lane 3' comments = 'Re#liable Friend!' )
  ( name = 'Vikrant Singh' city = 'Goa! Beach@' email = 'vikrant@singh#@example.com' address = 'Villa 22, P@alolem' comments = 'Social@ Worker#' )
  ( name = 'Neha Sharma' city = 'Ind@ore# MP!' email = 'neha@sharma@example.com' address = 'Home@ 98, New Town!' comments = 'Quick@ L!ear#ner' )
  ( name = 'Priya! Verma' city = 'De@lhi' email = 'priya.verma@example.com' address = 'Flat 12B, Lotus En@clave!' comments = 'Team, Play#er!' )
).

"Characters we want to remove from all fields
DATA: LT_UNWANTED TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF STRING WITH EMPTY KEY,
      LV_CHAR     TYPE STRING.

APPEND '@' TO LT_UNWANTED.
APPEND '#' TO LT_UNWANTED.
APPEND '!' TO LT_UNWANTED.
APPEND ',' TO LT_UNWANTED.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <FS_DATA>  TYPE ANY,
               <FS_FIELD> TYPE ANY.

"Loop through each row in the internal table
LOOP AT LT_DATA ASSIGNING <FS_DATA>.

  "Get runtime structure description using RTTI
  DATA(LO_TABLE_DESCR) = CAST CL_ABAP_TABLEDESCR( CL_ABAP_TYPEDESCR=>DESCRIBE_BY_DATA( LT_DATA ) ).
  DATA(LO_LINE_DESCR)  = CAST CL_ABAP_STRUCTDESCR( LO_TABLE_DESCR->GET_TABLE_LINE_TYPE( ) ).
  DATA(LT_COMPONENTS)  = LO_LINE_DESCR->COMPONENTS.

  "Loop through each field in the structure
  LOOP AT LT_COMPONENTS INTO DATA(LS_COMPONENT).
    ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_COMPONENT-NAME OF STRUCTURE <FS_DATA> TO <FS_FIELD>.

    "Replace all unwanted characters with space
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <FS_FIELD> IS ASSIGNED.
      LOOP AT LT_UNWANTED INTO LV_CHAR.
        REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF LV_CHAR IN <FS_FIELD> WITH ' '.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDLOOP.

"Display the cleaned internal table using SALV Grid
CALL METHOD CL_SALV_TABLE=>FACTORY
  IMPORTING
    R_SALV_TABLE = LT_FACTORTY
  CHANGING
    T_TABLE      = LT_DATA.

LT_FACTORTY->DISPLAY( ).

"Debugger breakpoint for inspection (optional)
BREAK-POINT.


Final Output after Replacing


ABAP Development

entry.title: Use of CONVERT KEY , which is return as a preliminary key %pid from Late Numbering
entry.content:
What is Covert Key ? – When we are using standard BDEF for our development, some time we can see standard BDEF using “Late Numbering” for creating preliminary key %pid.

For example, for PO Creation we use BDEF  I_PurchaseOrderTP_2 , for Purchase Contract BDEF is  I_PurchaseContractTP and for Service Entry Sheet BDEF r_serviceentrysheettp.

During CREATE operation using EML, in MAPPED parameter will only return a preliminary key %pid. Then to retrieve the final key in our application from this preliminary key we must use the CONVERT KEY key word.

This conversion will call in SAVE Sequence in SAVE_MODIFIED  method. And for this we need to use either Unmanaged Save or Additional Save.

Note:- If we want to save this convert key into the persistence of our own RAP business object, then we must use the unmanaged save approach.

For Example, here I am using BDEF r_serviceentrysheettp.

 

Scenario 1- When we are call EML inside our BDEF.

I have created an app where Service Entry Sheet will create when user click on Action Button.

I have used managed with unmanaged save implementation.

In Behavior Implementation class (Global Section) I have defined a variable that will hold the response of my API call.

In Action “createses” method

Here in variable mapped_serviceentry_sheet  we will hold the response of our API call i.e. MAPPED data.

In debugging we can see the %PID value.

Here we can see EML is returning only preliminary key, that we need to convert in SAVE_MODIFIED method.

In debugging -

 

Scenario 2 – Call EML outside BDEF or normal class.

In Debugging - 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Notas Fiscais não processadas após execução do job ED...
entry.content: Thank You Divyesh! But I didn´t find this screen parameter, to put " limit of the retrieval window ".
